content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 11,
1995
Andrea Ravas,
Esq.
Bay Area Rapid Transit District
P.O.
Box 12688
Oakland,
CA
94604—2688
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-091
Dear Ms.
Ravas:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the conflict—of—interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.
1
The advice I provided you is discussed
below.
OUESTIONS
1.
Is a position listed in Section 87200 required to be
included under any local government agency’s conflict of interest
code?
2.
Must an agency amend its code to delete any positions
designated under its conflict—of—interest code, which are
determined to be 721 filers as “other officials who manage public
investments” pursuant to Regulation 18720?
3.
Are officials who fall under the provisions of
Regulation 18720 considered “other public officials who manage
public investments” even though such officials may participate in
agency decisions other than those involving the management of
public investments?
4.
Should officials who are determined not to be “other
public officials who manage public investments” under the
definition of Regulation 18720 file Form 730?
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000
— 18954.
All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A—95—091
Page
2
CONCLUSIONS
1.
A position listed in Section 87200 is not required to be
included under any local government agency’s conflict of interest
code.
2.
An agency should amend its conflict of interest code to
delete any positions designated under the code which are
determined to be 721 filers as “other public officials who manage
public investments” pursuant to Regulation 18720.
However, there
is no prohibition in the Act for including positions, for
informational purposes, of officials who manage public investments
in the agency’s conflict of interest code.
3.
Yes.
Officials who are within the scope of Regulation
18720 are considered “other public officials who manage public
investments” even though they may participate in agency decisions
other than those involving the management of public investments.
4.
Yes.
These officials should file Form 730 if they are
subject to the conflict—of—interest disclosure requirements of
their agency’s conflict of interest code.
MALYSIS
You have asked various questions concerning “other public
officials who manage public investments.”
1.
Positions listed in Section 87200 are not required to be
included under any local government agency’s conflict of interest
code.
Section 87200 et seq., specifically requires specified public
officials to disclose their economic interests under the
provisions of the Political Reform Act.
These public officials
are required to disclose certain investments, interests in real
property and sources of income in their jurisdiction on a
statement of economic interests
(Form 721).
Section 87300 et seq., requires every agency to adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code designating those positions
within the agency which should similarly be required to disclose
their personal economic interests on a statement of economic
interests
(Form 730).
Section 87302 requires that every conflict
of interest code must specifically enumerate the positions within
the agency, other than those positions specified in Section 87200,
which involve the making or participation in the making of
decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any
financial interest.
Regulation 18730 contains all of the basic
provisions required by the Act.
Pursuant to Regulation 18730(b) (3),
a conflict of interest
code does not establish any disclosure obligation for persons who


Our File No. A-95-091
Page
3
are also listed in Section 87200 if they serve in essentially the
same capacity or if the geographical jurisdiction of the agency is
the same as or wholly included within the jurisdiction in which
those persons must report their financial interests under Section
87200.
Therefore,
a position listed in Section 87200 is not
required to be included under any local government agency’s
conflict of interest code as specified above.
2.
An agency should amend its code to delete any positions
which are determined to be 721 filers as “other public officials
who manage public investments” pursuant to Recrulation 18720.
Section 87200 includes other officials who manage public
investments.
An agency must now identify the officials who manage
public investments as described in Regulation 18720.
Obviously,
some of these officials are currently included in the agency’s
conflict of interest code.
As to these officials,
an agency
should amend its code to delete any positions designated under the
code, which are determined to be 721 filers as “other public
officials who manage public investments.”
However, there is no
prohibition in the Act for including positions, for informational
purposes, of officials who manage public investments in the
agency’s conflict of interest code.
Therefore,
we have advised
that a conflict of interest code may include a note listing the
positions which are determined by the agency to be positions which
manage public investments and will now file Form 721.
3.
Officials are “other public officials who manage public
investments” even though they may participate in other agency
decisions.
You also asked whether officials who fall under the
provisions of Regulation 18720 would be considered “other public
officials who manage public investments” even though such
officials may participate in agency decisions other than those
involving the management of public investments.
I indicated that,
regardless of their other duties, if the requirements of the
regulation are met these officials should file Form 721.
4.
Officials who are not 721 filers should file Form 730 if
they are sublect to the conflict-of-interest disclosure
requirements of their agency’s conflict of interest code.
Finally, you asked whether officials who do not meet the
narrow definition of Regulation 18720 should file Form 730,
provided their position participates in governmental decisions.
The comment to Regulation 18720 provides that individuals not
coming within the definition contained in Regulation 18720(b)
be subject to the conflict—of—interest disclosure requirements of
their agency’s conflict of interest code.
If this is the case,
those individuals must file a statement of economic interests


Our File No. A-95-091
Page 4
(Form 730).
(See also, Regulation 18700(a)
and Section 82019;
Randolph Advice Letter,
Our File No.
1-95-045.)
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division

Our File No.
A—95—091

3
are also listed in Section 87200 if they serve in essentially the

(Form 730).

and Section 82019;
